channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope following Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as he recovers from pneumonia in hospital
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis is participating in the Vatican’s weeklong spiritual retreat from a Rome hospital as he continued his recovery on Monday from double pneumonia and looks ahead to the 12th anniversary of his election amid questions about what the future of his papacy might look like.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian war veteran amputees learn adaptive skiing in Oregon
item.description: HOODOO SKI AREA, Ore. (AP) — Ukrainian war veteran Oleksandr Shvachka lost his left leg to Russian tank fire outside Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and JENNY KANE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Court-martial hearing set to begin for Pentagon leaker already facing years behind bars
item.description: BEDFORD, Mass. (AP) — Massachusetts Air National Guard member Jack Teixeira, who was sentenced last year to 15 years in prison for leaking highly classified military documents, is scheduled to begin his court-martial on additional charges Monday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's neighboring foreign ministers call for lifting sanctions and reconciliation
item.description: AMMAN, Jordan (AP) — Syria's top diplomat and his counterparts from neighboring countries Sunday called for the lifting of Western-led sanctions on Syria and post-war reconciliation.
item.dc:creator: OMAR AKOUR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chaos in Romania's capital after far-right Calin Georgescu barred from presidential redo
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Chaos broke out in Romania's capital Sunday evening as incensed supporters of the far-right populist Calin Georgescu protested the electoral body's decision to reject his candidacy in a presidential election redo.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mark Carney to become Canada’s next prime minister after Liberal Party vote
item.description: Canada’s Liberal Party chose its next leader in Mark Carney, and he will soon replace Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to lead the country. The exact date for Carney, 59, to take office is uncertain, but the timing will be decided by the incoming and outgoing leaders.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Russian forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian troops from the rear in Kursk
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Russian special forces walked inside a gas pipeline to strike Ukrainian units from the rear in the Kursk region, Ukraine’s military and Russian war bloggers reported, as Moscow claimed fresh gains in its push to recapture parts of the border province that Kyiv seized in a shock offensi
item.dc:creator: JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem aware Mexican, Canadian leaders have own 'political environments,' but Trump 'means business' on tariffs
item.description: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem said on Sunday that she knows Mexican and Canadian leaders have their own “political environments” but that President Trump “means business” on tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Syria's worst violence in months reopens wounds of the civil war
item.description: An ambush on a Syrian security patrol by gunmen loyal to ousted leader Bashar Assad escalated into clashes that a war monitor estimates have killed more than 1,000 people over four days.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide. Now thousands are detained on the Myanmar border
item.description: Thousands of sick, exhausted and terrified young men and women, from countries all over the world squat in rows, packed shoulder to shoulder, surgical masks covering their mouths and, some, their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Jintamas Saksornchai And Martha Mendoza, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Johnson says Zelensky has done 'about-face' on mineral deal
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said during a radio interview that Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has done a complete “about-face” on the U.S.-Ukraine minerals deal that was not signed after a heated meeting between the two delegations late last month.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Macron's diplomatic comeback: from France's domestic crisis to reshaping Europe's defense
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron is back at the center of global diplomacy, seeking to ease relations with President Donald Trump, championing a Ukraine peace plan alongside his British counterpart, and seeing his longstanding desire to boost European defense turning into reality.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rohingya refugees in Bangladesh brace for upcoming food reductions as aid agencies cut funding
item.description: COX’S BAZAR, Bangladesh (AP) — Rohingya refugees in crammed Bangladeshi camps say they are worried about a U.S.
item.dc:creator: SHAFIQUR RAHMAN and JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iran's supreme leader rejects nuclear talks with US after Trump's overtures
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader, said in a series of X posts Saturday he would not negotiate a nuclear deal with the U.S. due to its demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iranian leader rejects Trump’s push for nuclear talks
item.description: Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei rejected President Trump’s push for nuclear deal talks between the two nations, arguing the initiative is only a gateway for Washington to impose new demands and limit Tehran’s military capabilities. “Some coercive governments insist on negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: International Women's Day protests demand equal rights and an end to discrimination, sexual violence
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Women took to the streets of cities across Europe, Africa, South America and elsewhere to mark International Women’s Day with demands for ending inequality and gender-based violence.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill more than 20 as Polish prime minister warns against appeasement
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched heavy aerial attacks on Ukraine for a second night Saturday after the United States stopped sharing satellite images with the Ukrainian government, officials said. At least 22 people have been killed. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea unveils its first nuclear-powered submarine
item.description: North Korea revealed its first nuclear-powered submarine, posing a threat to the United States and South Korea. Kim Jong Un has inspected its construction progress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Impeached South Korean leader freed from prison
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison Saturday after a court ruled his detention over insurrection charges was invalid. The conservative president faces charges for his December effort to impose martial law that thrust the country into chaos.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan legislation seeks to rein in Erdogan's Turkey over ties to US foes
item.description: A bipartisan group of congressmen have taken issue with NATO ally Turkey aligning its foreign policy interests against the U.S. and its Western allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol freed from prison after canceled arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday after a court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia attacked Ukrainian energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones during the night, officials said Friday, hobbling the country’s ability to deliver heat and light to its citizens and to power weapons factories vital to its defenses.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The Latest: Trump considering pressure on Russia and seeking deal with Iran
item.description: President Donald Trump said he’s “strongly considering” sanctions and tariffs on Russia in hopes of forcing a settlement to the war in Ukraine. Trump also sent a letter to Iran’s leaders seeking to negotiate a nuclear deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis still receiving oxygen therapy, will not read Sunday prayers: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still receiving oxygen therapy and will not be able to read Sunday prayers, according to the Vatican. The pontiff is now entering his fourth week in Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump NATO stance shows 'simple-minded view of the world': Bolton
item.description: Trump said the U.S. may not help defend NATO members that fail to meet their military spending commitment.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Schools closed, public transport halted as rare cyclone approaches Australia
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred is forecast to cross the Queensland state coast somewhere between the Sunshine Coast region and the city of Gold Coast to the south early Saturday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kabul airport bombing suspect 'will die in prison': Sebastian Gorka
item.description: Sharifullah is charged in federal court in Virginia with providing material support to a foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Pope increases physical therapy, marks start of Lent from hospital
item.description: During the morning he participated in an Ash Wednesday celebration, receiving ashes and Holy Communion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Middle East latest: Arab countries are working on a postwar plan for Gaza
item.description: Arab foreign ministers met in Cairo on Monday for talks focusing on Egypt's plan to rebuild the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, hoping to counter President Donald Trump’s proposal to expel Palestinians from the coastal enclave and take it over.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A driver rams a car into crowd in Germany's Mannheim, leaving 2 dead and 11 injured
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver rammed a car into a crowd Monday in the southwestern German city of Mannheim, and authorities said two people were killed and 11 others injured, five of them severely.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump and his allies lash out at Ukraine’s Zelenskyy and suggest he may need to resign
item.description: President Donald Trump ’s senior aides and allies lashed out at Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy from Washington as he attended a European summit in London to rally international support for his military’s fight against the Russian invasion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
